issue_title: Bluetooth: Host: Support receiving long advertising data
issue_body:
**Is your enhancement proposal related to a problem? Please describe.**
I want to be able to receive 1650 bytes of advertising data as supported by a given Bluetooth Controller. Currently the host forwards all advertising reports directly to the application regardless of the data status. Therefore, if the advertiser is transmitting more bytes than can fit into a report, the application is unable to decode the data as it does not have the context describing if the data is first, intermediate, or last.

The `data_status` is present in both HCI LE Extended Advertising Report event (Core v5.3, Vol 4, Part E, Section 7.7.65.13) and HCI LE Periodic Advertising Report event (Core v5.3, Vol 4, Part E, Section 7.7.65.15) 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
To simplify the application layer design, I would like the host to recombine the received advertising data. The data shall be forwarded once the controller has indicated that there is no more data to follow. 

This requires the host to allocate a scan buffer to store the intermediate received advertising data. If the host supports receiving periodic advertising data, it requires one more.

Once intermediate advertising data has been received, this data shall be copied over to the scan buffer allocated in the host.

The size of the scan buffers should be configurable.

Implemented in https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/37377

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Instead of recombining advertising data in the host, this can be done in the application. That requires the data status to be forwarded. This approach is more error prone than letting the host recombine the data. If the application forgets to validate the data status flags, it may try to parse an intermediate fragment as if the data is complete.

Forwarding the data status to the application is implemented in https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/pull/37336.
pr_title: Bluetooth: Host: Reassemble extended advertising reports
pr_body:
The host reassmbles incomplete extended advertising reports
before presenting them to the application. Hereby we avoid
giving unparsable, partial reports to the application.

Important: The controller shall not discard these events in
HCI layer. If events with data status set to "No more to come"
are dropped, unrelated events will be concatinated.

The incoming HCI LE Extended Advertising Reports are stored
in a temporary buffer of size CONFIG_BT_EXT_SCAN_BUF_SIZE.

If either the controller or host is unable to receive the complete
advertisement, the property BT_GAP_ADV_PROP_REPORT_TRUNCATED
will be set.

If the host is truncating the data, and the controller indicates
it will provide more, the host will discard those events.

Fixes: https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/37368

Signed-off-by: Rubin Gerritsen <rubin.gerritsen@nordicsemi.no>
